records.ety: [OE. shoppe, schoppe, AS. sceoppa a treasury, a storehouse, stall, booth; akin to scypen a shed, LG. schup a shed, G. schoppen, schuppen, a shed, a coachhouse, OHG. scopf.]

defs: A building or an apartment in which goods, wares, drugs, etc., are sold by retail.
quotes: From shop to shop Wandering, and littering with unfolded silks The polished counter.

defs: A building in which mechanics or artisans work; as, a shoe shop; a car shop.
quotes: A tailor called me in his shop.

defs: A person's occupation, business, profession, or the like, as a subject of attention, interest, conversation, etc.; -- sometimes in deprecation or disapproval; as, to talk shop at a party. Also used attributively, as in shop talk.
A place where any industry is carried on; as, a chemist's shop;
any of the various places of business which are commonly called offices, as of a lawyer, doctor, broker, etc.
Any place of resort, as one's house, a restaurant, etc.
the group of workers and the activities controlled by an administrator; as, to have five people in one's shop.
notes: ☞ Shop is often used adjectively or in composition; as, shop rent, or shop-rent; shop thief, or shop-thief; shop window, or shop-window, etc.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Shopped (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Shopping.]

defs: To visit shops for the purpose of purchasing goods.
quotes: He was engaged with his mother and some ladies to go shopping.
